channel.title: Gabriele D'Angelo on Danilo Pianini
channel.description: Recent content in Gabriele D'Angelo on Danilo Pianini

item.title: First-order optimization algorithms: state of the art, classification, and performance: a practitioner's guide
item.description:
Abstract

Driven by the rising interest in machine learning techniques, mathematical continuous optimization algorithms have made great progress. Among them, first-order optimization algorithms, which rely on the first derivative (gradient) to find a function’s minimum or maximum, have gained popularity due to their efficiency and scalability. As a result, a vast number of optimization algorithms have been developed, each applying different techniques and offering diverse guarantees. This variety, while beneficial, makes selecting the most appropriate algorithm a challenging yet crucial task—choosing the wrong one may lead to sub-par accuracy or performance.
